background: Analyze the distribution of the residuals in the grandfather clock example, both before and after the outlier residual is removed. Determine whether the assumption of a normally distributed error term is reasonable.

task.query: Construct a histogram and normal probability plot for the two sets of residuals, both before and after the outlier residual is removed.
task.answer: A histogram and normal probability plot for the two sets of residuals are constructed using Minitab and are shown in Figures 12.45 and 12.46.

task.query: Analyze the histogram and normal probability plot to determine the impact of the outlier on the distribution of residuals.
task.answer: Note that the outlier appears to skew the histogram in Figure 12.45, whereas the histogram in Figure 12.46 appears to be more mound-shaped. Similarly, the pattern of residuals in the normal probability plot in Figure 12.46 (outlier deleted) is more nearly a straight line than the pattern in Figure 12.45 (outlier included).

task.query: Determine whether the assumption of a normally distributed error term is reasonable after removing the outlier.
task.answer: Thus, the normality assumption appears to be more plausible after the outlier is removed.
